onboarding.title: Get started with LIFT
onboarding.celebration_desc: LIFT is ready for your admissions cycle.
onboarding.step1_desc: Set up your grade bands, timeline, and candidate experience.
onboarding.step2_label: Invite an evaluator to your team
onboarding.step2_desc: Evaluators review candidate sessions and make recommendations.
onboarding.step3_desc: Send a secure link to a test candidate — even a colleague works.
onboarding.step4_desc: Have your test candidate complete the experience end to end.
onboarding.step5_desc: See what your evaluators will see — the TRI score, dimensions, and briefing.
help.school_admin.title: School Admin Guide
help.school_admin.subtitle: Everything you need to run your school's admissions process on LIFT.
help.dashboard.title: Dashboard Overview
help.dashboard.desc: Your dashboard gives you a real-time snapshot of your admissions pipeline. Here's what each section shows:
help.dashboard.stat_candidates: All candidates imported or invited to your school across all cycles. Includes active, completed, and flagged candidates.
help.dashboard.stat_sessions: Number of candidates who finished all assessment tasks. Each completed session automatically triggers the AI pipeline to generate insight profiles, TRI scores, and evaluator briefings.
help.dashboard.stat_flagged: Candidates whose AI-generated profiles were flagged for human review. This happens when confidence scores are low, unusual patterns are detected, or Learning Support Signals exceed thresholds. Always review these before making admissions decisions.
help.dashboard.stat_completion: The average percentage of tasks completed across all sessions. A number below 70% may indicate that candidates are dropping off mid-session — consider checking session settings or task difficulty.
help.dashboard.tip_review: The Review Queue shows candidates needing attention. Click any name to see their full profile, AI scores, and evaluator briefing.
help.candidates.title: Managing Candidates
help.candidates.import_1: Click "Import Excel" in the top right of the Candidates page.
help.candidates.import_2: Upload a spreadsheet (.xlsx or .csv) with columns: First Name, Last Name, Email, Grade Applying To, Date of Birth.
help.candidates.import_3: Review the import preview — LIFT will show you how many candidates will be created.
help.candidates.import_4: Click "Import" to create all candidates at once.
help.candidates.import_5: Each candidate gets an invite token automatically. Use "Invite Candidate" to send emails.
help.candidates.invite_1: Click "Invite Candidate" from the Candidates page.
help.candidates.invite_2: Fill in the candidate's name, email, grade applying to, and guardian info (if COPPA mode is on).
help.candidates.invite_3: Click "Send Invite" — the candidate receives an email with a secure session link.
help.candidates.invite_4: The link is valid for the number of days configured in your cycle settings.
help.candidates.status_invited: Invite sent, waiting for candidate to open the link
help.candidates.status_active: Session in progress or ready to begin
help.candidates.status_completed: All tasks finished, AI pipeline has run
help.candidates.status_flagged: Requires human review before admissions decision
help.candidates.demo_tip: When you first register, LIFT creates 3 demo candidates so you can explore the platform. These are marked with "(Demo)" and can be deleted anytime.
help.cycles.title: Admissions Cycles
help.cycles.desc: An admissions cycle represents one application period (e.g. "Fall 2026"). Each cycle has its own candidate pool, dates, and grade bands.
help.cycles.create_1: Click "New Cycle" from the Cycles page.
help.cycles.create_2: Enter a name (e.g. "Fall 2026 Admissions") and academic year.
help.cycles.create_3: Set opening and closing dates — invites can only be sent during this window.
help.cycles.create_4: Configure grade bands (6-7, 8, 9-11) — each band gets its own set of age-appropriate assessment tasks.
help.cycles.create_5: Click "Create Cycle" — it becomes active immediately.
help.cycles.tip: You can have multiple cycles but only one active at a time. Completed cycles are archived and their data remains accessible for reporting.
help.team.title: Team Management
help.team.desc: Add evaluators, interviewers, and other staff to your school. Each role sees a different view of the platform.
help.team.role_admin: Full control: candidates, cycles, team, settings, billing. You are this role.
help.team.role_evaluator: Reviews candidate profiles, TRI scores, AI briefings. Writes evaluator reviews and recommendations.
help.team.role_interviewer: Conducts interviews using AI-generated briefings. Submits rubric scores and interview notes.
help.team.invite_2: Enter their name, email, and select a role.
help.team.invite_3: They receive an email with a link to set up their account.
help.team.invite_4: Once they log in, they see only the pages relevant to their role.
help.team.warning_seats: Evaluator and interviewer seats are limited by your subscription tier. Check your plan limits in Settings → Subscription.
help.evaluator_workspace.title: Understanding the Evaluator View
help.evaluator_workspace.desc: When a candidate completes their session, LIFT generates an insight profile that evaluators use for review.
help.evaluator_workspace.tri_desc: A composite score (0-100) across 6 dimensions: reading, writing, reasoning, reflection, persistence, and support-seeking. Labels: Emerging (<40), Developing (40-59), Ready (60-79), Thriving (80+).
help.evaluator_workspace.dimensions_desc: Individual scores (0-100) for each dimension, generated by AI analyzing the candidate's actual responses. Each includes a confidence level and rationale.
help.evaluator_workspace.briefing_desc: An AI-generated pre-interview guide with 3-5 observations, 6-8 tailored interview questions, and areas to explore further.
help.evaluator_workspace.lss_desc: Behavioral patterns that may indicate a need for learning support evaluation. Three levels: None (0-1 flags), Watch (2-3), Recommend Screening (4+). This is NOT a diagnosis.

candidates.name: Name
session.write_here: Write your response here. Take your time...
session.show_work: Show your work — explain how you got your answer
session.explain_thinking: Explain your thinking step by step...
session.no_wrong_answers: There are no right or wrong answers. Share your honest thoughts.
settings.language_desc: Language for invites and session UI
settings.coppa_desc: Require guardian consent for all new invites
settings.session_pause_desc: Allow candidates to pause and resume sessions
settings.data_retention_desc: How long candidate data is kept
settings.human_review_desc: Flag every insight profile for manual review
settings.voice_response_desc: Allow candidates to speak their responses instead of typing. Recommended for Grade 6-7.
settings.passage_reader_desc: Read passages aloud to candidates. Helps with reading difficulties without affecting scoring.
settings.audio_privacy: Voice recordings are transcribed and immediately deleted. No audio is stored after transcription.
analytics.candidates_info: Total candidates imported or invited this cycle
analytics.sessions_info: Candidates who finished all assessment tasks
analytics.rate_info: Percentage of invited candidates who completed their session
analytics.tri_info: Average Transition Readiness Index across all completed sessions
analytics.usage_info: Sessions consumed vs your plan limit
analytics.time_info: Average time candidates spend completing all tasks
